zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Catherine Holme於1544年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾梅時，她的父親John Holme18歲，母親Ann Aislaby16歲。她於1561年與Sir Marmaduke Constable在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1634年6月8日在英國林可爾史爾格爾享年90歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾約克約克 明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Holme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northwestern England): variant of   Holm   and of   Home   3 and 4.
Danish and Swedish: variant of   Holm   1.

variants.name: Holm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

variants.name: Holmes
meanings: English: either from the plural form of Holme , or else a variant of Holme or Home , with excrescent -s (see Holm ).

Scottish: probably a habitational name from Holmes near Dundonald (Ayrshire), or possibly from another place so called in the barony of Inchestuir (Angus). Both placenames likely derive from the plural form of Middle English, Older Scots holm ‘islet, raised land in a marsh’ (see Holm ).

Scottish and Irish: adopted for Scottish Gaelic and Irish Mac Thómais, Mac Thómais (see McComb ). In parts of western Ireland, Holmes is also a variant of Cavish, from Gaelic Mac Thámhais, another patronymic from Thomas . Early bearers in Ireland were probably immigrants from Scotland.

nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Catherine Holme was born in 1544, in Holme, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Holme, was 18 and her mother, Ann Aislaby, was 16. She married Sir Marmaduke Constable in 1561, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 8 June 1634, in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 90, and was buried in York Minster, York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Sir Marmaduke Constable en 1561, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 8 juin 1634, à Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 90 ans, et a été enterrée à York Minster, York, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.York Minster, York, York Unitary Authori: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 요르크 요르크 민스터
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Catherine Holme(이)가 1544년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Holme(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Aislaby(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1561년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Marmaduke Constable(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1634년 6월 8일 영국 링코실르 고스힐에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 요르크 요르크 민스터에 묻혔다.

ru.York Minster, York, York Unitary Authori: ерк минстир, ерк, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Catherine Holme родилась в 1544 г. в местности холм, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Holme было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Ann Aislaby, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Marmaduke Constable в 1561 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 8 июня 1634 г. в местности гоксхилл, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 90 лет и была похоронена в местности ерк минстир, ерк, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
